track: 3 — recall/discovery (Shape-B: precision / silence on real off-mainstream code)
shape: B (precision-silence — no defect; the value is the named suppressions on a clean repo)

repo.name: kemalcr/kemal
repo.stack: Crystal (web framework) — off-mainstream: near-zero const-fold / dead-code / cross-module tooling exists for it (the moat)
repo.size: 33 files, 1923 code LOC (scc), 1 component (the `Kemal` library module)

result_headline: DeepInit's deterministic detector suite raises ZERO issues on kemal — every structural near-candidate is suppressed by a NAMED guard. A clean Shape-B scope-honesty result on real Crystal (comprehension, not keyword-matching).

per_family.suppression_reason: Both conditions are RUNTIME method calls (`.includes?(path)` / `.has_key?(key)`) on a constant collection — NOT `if CONST` where the const folds to a compile-time literal boolean. The IF-10 predicate clause C4 (NAME is the whole test, foldable to a literal) is FALSE, so the detector correctly stays silent. A naive 'a constant appears in the condition → dead branch' detector would over-fire on both.

file_upload's rescue does `cleanup; raise ex` — it RE-RAISES (not swallowed). param_parser's rescue returns the raw `value` — a DELIBERATE fallback for a malformed URL param, in a `private` method. Additionally, kemal is a SINGLE component (one library module), so the IF-7(c) moat gate (the empty handler must be consumed across a COMPONENT boundary where a caller can't see the failure) has no cross-component consumer to satisfy. Both near-candidates are suppressed (re-raise / deliberate-fallback + single-component moat). A naive `no-empty`/`rescue-without-reraise` linter would fire on param_parser.

near_candidates: src/kemal/dsl.cr:12 — `HTTP_METHODS = %w[get post put patch delete options]` (6)
src/kemal/dsl.cr:13 — `FILTER_METHODS = %w[get post put patch delete options all]` (7, +`all`)
per_family.suppression_reason: The shipped IF-6 slice (§24) fires only on a set defined under the SAME name in ≥2 components with CONFLICTING membership. Here the three sets carry DIFFERENT names and serve different purposes: FILTER_METHODS is intentionally HTTP_METHODS + `all` (filters can target every method); ALLOWED_METHODS is the distinct '_method override' verb set (uppercase). Different-named sets with intentional membership differences are the fully-semantic same-logic-no-shared-name sub-case, which is DEFERRED (, no decidable join key). The detector correctly stays silent. A naive 'two method lists differ → divergence' set-diff would over-fire on HTTP_METHODS vs FILTER_METHODS.

near_candidates: single-component library (the `Kemal` module) — no cross-component import graph
per_family.suppression_reason: Not applicable: both families require ≥2 components. kemal is one library module, so there is no cross-component cycle or silent shared-resource coupling to detect (a correct N/A, recorded as a coverage degrade `no-cross-module-imports-IF-3a-not-applicable` / `no-import-cycle-IF-8-not-applicable`).

Measured in : the literal rule is followed by ZERO shipped handlers (every handler `include HTTP::Handler` instead) → the census returns STALE (a neutral rule-health flag for a human), not 12 auto-judgments. See validation/results/kemalcr-kemal.json.

naive_vs_guarded_illustrative.note: Illustrative (looser than the Run-16 class-census — these are detector-pattern near-misses, not a rigorously enumerated class). A naive structural detector keying on surface patterns would over-fire on ~4 sites (2 IF-10 runtime-calls + 1 IF-7c deliberate-fallback + 1 IF-6 different-named set-diff); the guarded suite raises 0, each suppression named.

honest_assessment: kemal is a mature, clean single-component framework, so 0 raised issues is the correct precision outcome — there is nothing to recall here (this is a Shape-B scope-honesty datapoint, NOT a recall measurement). The value is twofold: (1) it exercises DeepInit's deterministic detector suite end-to-end on real CRYSTAL source — an off-mainstream language with near-zero const-fold/dead-code tooling (the moat) — and the suite runs cleanly, suppressing every near-candidate by a NAMED structural guard rather than over-firing; (2) it corroborates the 'reasons about your code, not just patterns' claim on real code (re-raise vs swallow, runtime-call vs const-fold, different-named-by-design vs same-name-divergent). No cost ledger is recorded because a direct detector sweep is not a representative full Detect→Emit skill-run; the  cost  needs a real full-pipeline run with measured telemetry.
